_comment: Momentum trajectories for Wang Family Village (Art XXXIX). Each trajectory defines: current state, rate of change, drivers, thresholds, consequences. The world is mid-sentence when the player arrives. These trajectories advance every simulation tick regardless of player presence (Art V). When Java wires this data into the WorldStateEngine or ActorTickLoop, the world gains Evolution (Gold-Standard dim 10) and passes the Momentum Test (Art XXXIX.5: 3+ systems visibly mid-trajectory at player arrival).

_design_notes.no_player_dependency: All drivers advance independently. The player may observe consequences but does not cause them.
_design_notes.observable_at_arrival: When the player enters zhao_plains, all three trajectories are at mid-point. The player sees the effects (NPC dialogue about declining harvests, wolf tracks closer to village, Teng servants demanding more) without any script firing.
_design_notes.threshold_consequences: When a threshold is crossed, the consequence changes the world state permanently. This is not reset. Crossing 'wolf_attack' threshold spawns wolves that kill the dog. The dog stays dead. This is the Memory Metric (Art XXXI.5).
_design_notes.consumption_by_existing_systems: Java should read this file from WorldStateDataLoader (existing) or a new lightweight reader. The trajectories feed into: (1) NPC desire urgency modulation (spirit vein decline raises Wang Tianshan's warn desires), (2) VillageBeastActivity intensity scaling (wolf pressure increases event frequency), (3) NPC dialogue topic injection (Teng pressure generates rumor desires). No new Engine required (Art XXVI).

trajectories.name: Wang Family Village Spirit Vein Thinning
trajectories.description: The village sits on a minor spirit vein that has been degrading for decades. This is the root cause of the village's decline: poorer harvests, weaker villagers, less resistance to the Teng family. In canon, the village's poverty is the backdrop to Wang Lin's entire early life. The elder knows but cannot fix it. A cultivator might — but none will waste effort on a dying vein in a mortal village. EXPLICIT: village poverty is central to RI Ch.1-3.
trajectories.canon_fidelity: EXPLICIT — Wang Family Village is poor. The spirit vein's decline explains why. Elder Wang Tianshui's worry about the family's future is rooted in this ecological reality, not abstract concern.

thresholds.consequence: village harvest yield drops 40%. Wang Tianshui's spirit_vein_warning desire urgency increases to 0.8. NPC dialogue shifts to 'the crops are failing again.' Memory: crop_failure_year_N.

The vein collapses. No more spiritual energy. The village becomes completely mortal. EcosystemManager flora population drops to 0. This is irreversible without external intervention (a formation expert). Permanently changes the Ecology dimension. Memory: spirit_vein_collapsed.

drivers.name: natural_decay
drivers.description: The vein is old. Natural qi dissipation exceeds natural recharge. This is the primary driver.

drivers.name: cultivator_drain
drivers.description: NPC cultivators (Daoist Water, Qing Shui) draw qi for their own cultivation. Minor but cumulative.

drivers.name: formation_stabilization
drivers.description: If a player or NPC repairs the village shrine's formation fragment, the decay rate halves. This is a concrete player action with lasting consequence.

observable_effects: Wang Tianshui's 'spirit_vein_warning' desire urgency scales with vein decline: 0.60 at current 0.35, 0.80 at crop_failure threshold.
Wang Tianshan's 'village_decline' rumor desire activates when qi < 0.30.
EcosystemManager flora population coefficient scales linearly with qi_density/0.7.
PerceptionTier >= qi_condensation: player can see qi fluctuation colors in the village well.
NPCs with memory access may say 'The well doesn't sparkle like it used to.' (memory-driven dialogue, 076).
trajectories.momentum_test_role: VISIBLE AT ARRIVAL. The player enters the village and hears NPCs mention poor harvests (desire system) and sees the spirit vein effects if they have cultivation perception. No script needed — the desire system and memory system already reference the vein.

trajectories.name: Wolf Pack Pressure on Wang Family Village
trajectories.description: Spirit wolves from the surrounding hills have been encroaching on village territory for years. As their natural prey (spirit rabbits) decline (linked to spirit vein thinning), they push closer to the village. This creates the canonical danger backdrop: Da Niu spots tracks (his desire), Wang Qingyue warns children (her desire), the dog may die defending the village. INFERRED: wolves are mentioned throughout RI Ch.1-5 as a constant background threat in the Zhao Country wilderness.
trajectories.canon_fidelity: INFERRED — RI Ch.1-5 establishes the wilderness around Zhao Country as dangerous. Wang Lin encounters beasts repeatedly. The wolf threat to the village is a reasonable inference from the ecology. Da Niu's 'wolf_sighting' desire (081) is directly canon-grounded.

current_state.link_mechanism: pressure_escalation += 0.0005 * (1 - spirit_vein.qi_density / spirit_vein.qi_density_max). As prey declines, wolves push closer.

thresholds.consequence: Wolf tracks appear within 32 blocks of village center. Da Niu's 'wolf_sighting' desire activates. VillageBeastActivity event frequency increases by 50%. NPCs with memory access mention 'strange tracks near the fields.' Memory: wolves_near_village.

A wolf pack attacks the south bend. If the hunting dog is there, it fights and may die (50% chance). This is the canonical Art XXXI.5 Memory Metric event: Old Chen's dog dies. Triggers memory chain: grief → retelling → 'Used to have one.' Memory: dog_died_wolf_attack.

Wolves are brazen enough to enter the village at night. NPC safety desires spike. Wang Tianshui considers requesting Heng Yue Sect aid. This is a late-game event that signals the village is becoming uninhabitable — canonically, Wang Lin leaves before this point.

drivers.name: prey_decline
drivers.description: As spirit vein thins, spirit rabbit population drops (linked via ecosystem). Wolves seek alternative food sources — village livestock, the dog, eventually people.

drivers.name: winter_hunger
drivers.description: In winter (days 80-120 of MC year), food scarcity pushes wolf pressure up by 50% temporarily.

drivers.name: player_hunting
drivers.description: If the player kills wolves near the village, pressure drops. The world reacts: fewer wolves, safer village, but the pack may retaliate or move to a different territory.

observable_effects: VillageBeastActivity event interval scales inversely with pressure: at 0.4, every 6000 ticks; at 0.7, every 3000 ticks; at 0.9, every 1500 ticks.
Da Niu's 'wolf_sighting' desire urgency = pressure_level. At 0.5, he says 'I saw tracks near the south bend!' (already fires via 083 NPC→NPC).
Wang Qingyue's 'warn_about_spirit_beasts' desire urgency scales with pressure. At 0.6, she approaches children and warns them.
When 'wolf_attack_south_bend' fires: WorldEventBus publishes catastrophe event. MemoryEventSubscriber records memories. Rumor chain begins. NPCs reference it for weeks.
The dog's death is PERMANENT. It does not respawn. This is the Memory Metric test (Art XXXI.5). The world remembers.
trajectories.momentum_test_role: VISIBLE AT ARRIVAL. At pressure 0.4, the player sees occasional wolf tracks (Da Niu mentions them). At 0.5+, VillageBeastActivity fires more often. The player HEARS wolves howling. No script — the beast activity system produces this.

trajectories.name: Teng Family Tribute Pressure on Wang Family Village
trajectories.description: The Teng family extracts tribute (grain, labor, timber) from surrounding villages. As Wang Family Village weakens (spirit vein decline, poorer harvests), the Teng family demands proportionally more to compensate. This creates an escalating oppression spiral: village weakens → Teng demands more → village weakens further. INFERRED: Teng family oppression is central to RI Ch.1-3. Wang Tianshui's desire about Teng grain purchases (083) confirms this dynamic.
trajectories.canon_fidelity: INFERRED — Teng family controls the region. Multiple NPC desires reference Teng oppression (Wang Tianshui warn_about_teng, Wang Zhou avoid_teng_servants, Wang Ping complain_teng_tax). The escalation spiral is a reasonable inference from the economic dynamics.

current_state.link_mechanism: As harvests decline (qi_density < 0.3), Teng increases demand percentage. tribute_level += 0.001 * (1 - qi_density/qi_density_max). The weaker the village, the more they take.

thresholds.consequence: Teng servants visit the village market daily (already in canon). Teng Huayuan's 'tribute_demand' desire is always active. Wang Zhou's 'avoid_teng_servants' desire activates. Memory: teng_servants_at_market.

Teng demands exceed 25% of harvest. Wang Tianshui's 'village_decline' rumor urgency increases. Multiple NPCs independently complain about Teng oppression. The circulating desire ecosystem makes the oppression FEEL systemic, not scripted.
Teng takes everything. Families begin leaving. This is the point of no return for the village — canonically, Wang Lin has already left for Heng Yue by this point. Memory: village_abandoned.

drivers.name: village_weakness
drivers.description: As the village weakens (linked to spirit vein), Teng increases demands. This is the escalation spiral.

drivers.name: teng_ambition
drivers.description: Teng Huayuan's personal ambition drives faster escalation. If he is present near the village, rate doubles.

drivers.name: player_resistance
drivers.description: If the player disrupts Teng operations (kills servants, steals tribute, confronts Teng Huayuan), tribute pressure drops. The world reacts: fewer servant visits, but Teng may send enforcers.

observable_effects: Teng Huayuan's 'tribute_demand' desire urgency scales with tribute_level. At 0.5, moderate; at 0.7, he is aggressive.
Wang Ping's 'complain_teng_tax' desire urgency scales with tribute_level. At 0.6, he actively seeks help.
Zhou Rui's 'complain_teng_tax_burden' desire activates at tribute_level 0.55.
The number of Teng servant entities spawned near the village scales with tribute_level (future Java wiring).
Wang Tianshui's 'warn_about_teng' desire urgency = max(0.55, tribute_level). The elder warns more urgently as pressure grows.
trajectories.momentum_test_role: VISIBLE AT ARRIVAL. At tribute 0.5, the player sees Teng servants at the market (Teng Huayuan's desire fires on proximity). NPCs mention Teng grain purchases. The oppression is ambient, not quest-triggered.

cross_trajectory_links.description: Spirit vein decline is the ROOT CAUSE. It drives both wolf pressure (prey decline) and Teng tribute (harvest decline). Fixing the vein (formation repair) slows ALL three trajectories. This makes the shrine formation repair (affordance: structure:wang_lin_childhood_home → examine_family_mark → repair) the single most impactful player action in Chapter 1.
cross_trajectory_links.mechanism: qi_density affects prey populations (ecosystem) and harvest yields (economy)

cross_trajectory_links.description: Wolf attacks create fear, which reduces NPC willingness to work fields, which reduces harvests, which increases Teng tribute pressure. A positive feedback loop of decline.
cross_trajectory_links.mechanism: Fear reduces labor efficiency → lower harvest → higher tribute demand

cross_trajectory_links.description: When all three trajectories cross their danger thresholds simultaneously, the village enters a crisis state. Multiple NPCs activate emergency desires. The player observes a village in freefall — which is exactly the atmosphere of Wang Lin's early life.
cross_trajectory_links.consequence: Wang Tianshui's 'village_decline' urgency maxes at 0.9. Multiple NPC warning desires fire in sequence. The player sees a village being squeezed from three sides simultaneously. This IS the experience of Renegade Immortal Chapter 1-3 — not because a script says so, but because three independent systems are all declining at once.

art_xxxix_5_momentum_test.description: Art XXXIX.5: 3+ systems visibly mid-trajectory at player arrival. When Java wires this data, ALL THREE trajectories are mid-point when the player enters. The player observes: (1) NPC dialogue about declining harvests (spirit vein), (2) wolf howls in the distance (wolf pressure), (3) Teng servants at the market (Teng tribute). No scripts fire. The world is in motion.
perceivable_without_notification: Player hears wolves howling (VillageBeastActivity, already exists).
Player sees Teng servants near market (Teng Huayuan desire fires on proximity, already exists).
Player overhears NPC→NPC desire: Wang Tianshui warns about the spirit vein (083 NPC→NPC broadcast, already exists).

art_xxxi_3_stronger_metric.description: Art XXXI.3: Could somebody who never read RI accidentally recreate Wang Lin's early life? The three trajectories produce: declining village (economy), wolf threat (danger), Teng oppression (conflict). The player who chops wood, fishes at the south bend, witnesses wolf tracks, and hears the elder worry is recreating Wang Lin's experience — through simulation, not scripting.
recreated_beats: Rumors about declining harvests (spirit vein → Teng tribute linkage)
Wolf sightings at south bend (wolf pressure → Da Niu desire)
Teng servants demanding grain (Teng tribute → multiple NPC desires)
Heng Yue recruiter testing youth (living_events, staged after wanderer)
The dog's death and village grief (wolf attack threshold → Memory Metric)

art_v_compliance.description: All three trajectories advance without the player. The spirit vein decays on its own. Wolves escalate on their own. Teng demands grow on their own. The player can INTERVENE (repair formation, kill wolves, fight Teng) which redirects trajectories (Art XXXIX.6). But if the player never exists, the village still declines.
